one_line_takeaway: AFTUNE makes cloud LLM fine-tuning and inference auditable by hashing only layer/step block-boundary states during full-speed GPU execution and later re-verifying client-sampled blocks inside a small CPU TEE, achieving ~19-21% overhead on 8-14B models (vs 75-83% for step-wise TEE) with float32 precision required to keep the numerical tolerance from being exploitable.
key_finding: Block-decomposed, decouple-execution-from-verification auditing achieves near-baseline overhead where full-TEE encapsulation is impractical, while giving strong probabilistic detection. At the default BL=4,BS=8 config, AFTUNE adds only 19% training overhead for Qwen2.5-14B and 21% for Llama-3.1-8B, versus 75% and 83% for the Step-TEE baseline, and cuts audit storage by over half (e.g., 213.5 GB vs 555.8 GB for Qwen per 2048 samples). For ViT-Large, Full-TEE training costs 3000% overhead vs AFTUNE's 26-71%. Sampling is efficient: verifying just 1% of blocks (m=10 of 1000) when 10% are compromised already yields ~65% detection, compounding toward certainty over repeated audits. Crucially, verification MUST use at least float32 precision: bfloat16 recomputation errors are large enough that adversarial activation perturbations fall within the tolerance band and evade detection, whereas float32 errors are 3-4 orders of magnitude smaller than the smallest successful attack perturbation, giving a clean detection margin. Larger blocks reduce overhead/storage but lengthen recomputation chains and raise numerical error.
core_contribution: AFTUNE, a framework that lets a client cryptographically audit whether a cloud provider actually performed the contracted fine-tuning AND inference on a (possibly proprietary, weights-hidden) LLM, without running the whole workload inside a TEE. Its central idea is 'block decomposition with compositional verification': the provider records and hashes only boundary states (activations and gradients at layer-block edges, parameters and optimizer state at step-block boundaries) organized into a 2D grid of layer x step blocks. Because internal states within a block are deterministic functions of the block's boundary states, each block is an independently, atomically re-verifiable unit whose boundary-continuity chains together into end-to-end verification. This is what the PAPER contributes; note that ZKP-based (zkLLM, zkGPT, VeriLoRA) and prior run-inside-TEE approaches are cited as related work the paper explicitly positions itself against, not as its own results. Two supporting mechanisms: a map-reduce-style GPU-parallel hashing scheme (chunk, parallel-hash, aggregate) to make commitment generation accelerator-friendly, and a sampling-based spot-check protocol giving probabilistic detection guarantees.
threat_model: A misbehaving or compromised cloud provider that operates the training/inference infrastructure and is trusted with confidential proprietary model weights, but may deviate from the contracted computation to cut cost or inject malice while evading detection. The adversary controls all software/infrastructure OUTSIDE the TEE and fully knows AFTUNE's design. Concrete provider misbehaviors (Appendix A): under-training (fewer epochs, truncated batches, skipped data), model substitution (weaker/distilled base or mid-training swap), backdoor/data-poisoning injection during training, serving a cheaper/wrong model at inference, and fabricated/cached/manipulated inference responses. Defender is the client, who supplies the dataset and hyperparameters but cannot see the weights. Trust anchors: a public hash commitment of the base model as the trusted initial checkpoint, and widely-available CPU TEEs with remote attestation. Explicitly out of scope: attacks on the TEE itself or on the cryptographic hash functions.
limitations: Explicitly stated: (1) TEEs are the root of trust, so a compromised TEE breaks all guarantees; (2) sampled verification gives only probabilistic, not deterministic, detection, so tampering can still evade with some probability. Additional constraints from the text: assumes no single parameter tensor exceeds TEE memory; sparse checkpointing requires deterministic recomputation, forcing non-deterministic layers (e.g., in DINOv2-Giant) into IC=1 blocks; the zero-storage mode costs a full extra training pass (100% overhead); Step-TEE/Full-TEE baselines were often infeasible to actually run in-TEE (Step-TEE verify times are CPU-measured and TEE time only estimated at 1.5-2x). Attacks on hash functions and TEE side-channels are out of scope. Evaluation is at the 8B-14B / ViT-Large scale on single-node RTX PRO 6000 GPUs, not at the multi-node sharded frontier scale the motivation invokes.

datasets_used: MentalChat16K (mental-health conversational dataset, used for LLM fine-tuning)
ImageNet-1K (used for vision model image classification fine-tuning)
quantitative_results: Qwen2.5-14B, BL=4/BS=8: AFTUNE 135.6s train time, 19% overhead, 213.5 GB storage, 20.3s prep, 58.0s verify/block (vs Baseline 114.1s; Step-TEE 199.2s, 75%, 555.8 GB)
Llama-3.1-8B, BL=4/BS=8: AFTUNE 76.1s, 21% overhead, 146.2 GB, 15.1s prep, 48.2s verify (vs Baseline 62.8s; Step-TEE 114.8s, 83%, 333.3 GB)
DINOv2-Giant, BL=8/BS=8: AFTUNE 44% overhead, 19.1 GB (vs Step-TEE 263% overhead, 272.3 GB)
ViT-Large: Full-TEE 3000% overhead, Step-TEE 167%; AFTUNE 26-71% overhead across configs (8.6-11.6s vs 6.8s baseline)
Sampling detection: 10% blocks compromised (k=100 of Nblocks=1000), verifying 1% (m=10) gives Pdetect ~= 65%
Inference (IA=4): 8-25% latency overhead, 1-5 MB storage per request, 5-15s verification per request (e.g., Qwen2.5-14B BL=4: 387ms, 8% overhead, 1.36 MB, 14.7s)
LoRA fine-tuning (rank r=64, Q/V projections): Llama-3.1-8B 16-17% overhead (~52.5-52.8s vs 45.1s); Qwen2.5-14B 14-15% overhead
Numerical precision: bfloat16 inference-verification activation errors ~1e-4 to 6e-3 (exploitable); float32 ~4e-8 to 8e-7 (3-4 orders smaller than min attack perturbation ~5e-5); float64 ~1e-16
Smallest successful attack perturbations (relative L2): parameter poisoning min ~5.3e-5; adversarial activation attacks min ~1.5e-6, both orders of magnitude above float32 recomputation error
baselines_compared: Baseline: standard training/inference without verification (performance upper bound)
Full TEE: entire training executed inside a TEE (only feasible for ViT-Large; 3000% overhead)

Step TEE: TEE verification of one full training step across all layers at a time, lacking AFTUNE's 2D block granularity (could not actually run in-TEE for larger models due to memory; CPU times reported, TEE estimated at 1.5-2x)
